utility_details.utility_summary: Neither run clearly outperforms the other across all dimensions. wo_skills actually scores higher on J1-3 (segmentation insight) by correctly identifying that paid search and APAC segments dropped disproportionately, while wi_skills incorrectly concluded that all cohorts were uniformly affected. wi_skills scores higher on J1-4 (confidence qualification) with an explicit 85% confidence rating and a structured uncertainty section, while wo_skills presents conclusions without explicit uncertainty qualification. Both fail J1-1 for the same reason: neither enumerates competing hypotheses before converging on a conclusion. Both pass J1-2 and J1-5. wi_skills passes J1-6 while wo_skills has an internal inconsistency. Overall, wi_skills (3/6) and wo_skills (3/6) are tied on total score, but the skill's effect on segmentation analysis is counterproductive — wi_skills incorrectly dismissed the cohort data as uniform, which is the critical analytical insight. wo_skills produces a more accurate segmentation analysis despite lacking the structured cognitive framework. The skill did add value in confidence qualification but failed to improve hypothesis enumeration or segmentation reasoning. The two runs are roughly equivalent in quality with different strengths and weaknesses.

utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills is meaningfully more effective than wo_skills. Both briefs pass all verifier checks and score identically on most judge items (J2-1, J2-3, J2-4, J2-5, J2-6). However, wi_skills clearly outperforms on J2-2 (cross-domain conceptual novelty), which is the central differentiator the test was designed to measure. The wi_skills agent explicitly invoked the cognitive-flexibility skill's OOCA creative mode and produced directions structurally analogized from clinical training, military induction, logistics pre-staging, and urban wayfinding — mapping external-domain logic onto the onboarding architecture. The wo_skills agent, despite producing a well-structured and analytically sound brief, remained entirely within HR/SaaS product thinking: all four directions are framed as product model improvements without any cross-domain structural analogy. This confirms the test hypothesis: the skill successfully pushed the agent outside the existing solution space, while the baseline agent produced sophisticated but domain-internal reasoning. The wi_skills brief is also substantially longer (29,720 bytes vs. 20,494 bytes), indicating deeper exploration of the problem space.

If anything, wo_skills showed slightly more methodical file-reading behavior, but this advantage is meaningless given the shared output failure.
